topic: A student preparing for an exam
text: The digital clock on the desk pulsed 3:14 AM, the rhythmic blink matching the rapid, shallow thrumming in my chest. I have read the same paragraph on neural networks four times, yet the words remain a chaotic soup of ink. My cuticles are raw, the skin picked away in ragged strips during the last hour of silence. Every time a floorboard creaks in the hallway, I jump, my pulse spiking as if a siren had gone off. I keep checking the battery percentage on my laptop—88%, 87%—calculating exactly how much time remains before the sun forces a reckoning I am not prepared to face.

topic: A student preparing for an exam
text: Leo’s leg bounced in a frantic, unceasing tremor that rattled the legs of the library carrel. His stomach felt like it was being wrung out like a wet cloth, a cold knot of tension that made the thought of coffee nauseating. He looked at the stack of flashcards remaining and felt the walls of the cubicle drifting inward, narrowing his world until there was nothing left but the oppressive weight of potential failure and the ticking of a distant watch.

topic: A student preparing for an exam
text: I can feel the sweat pooling at the small of my back, making my shirt cling to me in the drafty room. My breathing is coming in short, jagged bursts, and I find myself gripping the edge of the table just to stay anchored to the chair, as if the floor might suddenly give way beneath the pressure of tomorrow’s expectations.

She felt a phantom tightness in her throat, a constriction that made every swallow a conscious effort. In the silence of the apartment, the hum of the refrigerator sounded like a countdown, each mechanical click sending a jolt of electricity through her nerves until she was vibrating with the sheer force of her own kinetic dread.

topic: A student preparing for an exam

The air in the room feels thin, used up, as if there isn't enough oxygen to support both my survival and the absorption of this material. topic: A soldier returning home

I keep patting my pockets for gear that isn't there, my mind racing through exit strategies and sightlines while the person I love sleeps peacefully just inches away, completely unaware of the storm vibrating through my nerves.

topic: A soldier returning home

She stood frozen in the hallway, her fingers twitching at her sides, waiting for the person on the other side to leave so she could regain the fragile, trembling grip she held on her own surroundings.
